---
title: "Plot Heaps' Law Curves for PubMed Abstracts Case Study"
author: "Uyen 'Rachel' Lai and Paul Sheridan"
output: html_notebook
---

# Preliminaries
```{r}
# Delete all variables initialized in R session, if any
rm(list = ls())

# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(reticulate)
library(magicaxis)
require(broom)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)
library(latex2exp)
library(grid)

# Set plot colors 
gg_color_hue = function(n, alpha = 1) {
  hues <- seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
  hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100, alpha = alpha)[1 : n]
}
cols <- c("grey25", gg_color_hue(n = 3))
tcols <- c("grey25", gg_color_hue(n = 3, alpha = 0.5))
gray25 <- cols[1]; red <- cols[2]; green <- cols[3]; blue <- cols[4]
tred <- cols[2]; tgreen <- tcols[3]; tblue <- tcols[4]

# Custom color scheme
hex <- hue_pal()(3)
gg_red <- hex[1]
gg_green <- hex[2]
gg_blue <- hex[3]
gg_orange <- brewer.pal(n = 11, name = "PuOr")[4]
gg_purple <- "#C77CFF"
```

# Define Heaps' law

```{r}
heaps_law <- function(x, alpha, beta) {
alpha * x ^ beta
}

COLORS <- c(
"pubmed"     = gg_red,
"gptneo125m" = gg_blue,
"gptneo1.3b" = gg_green,
"gptneo2.7b" = gg_orange
)
```



# Open vocabulary setting

```{r}
# this is hard code value that take from file_path  llm_and_human_heapslaw_stats.csv
# Hard code estimate alpha and beta values

# PubMed Abstracts
alpha_hat <- round(24.5378 , 4)
beta_hat <- round(0.5418, 4)
vocab_size <- 153889
total_word <- 10442049
  
# GPT-Neo 125m
alpha_hat1 <- round(7.1831, 4)
beta_hat1 <- round(0.6033, 4)
vocab_size1 <- 108309
total_word1 <- 8558267

# GPT-Neo 1.3B
alpha_hat2 <- round(14.2471, 4)
beta_hat2 <- round(0.5486 , 4)
vocab_size2 <- 102661
total_word2 <- 11027223

# GPT-Neo 2.7B
alpha_hat3 <- round(19.5698 , 4)
beta_hat3 <- round(0.5367 , 4)
vocab_size3 <- 112593
total_word3 <- 10248310

# Hard code collection size and vocab size limits
n_max <- max(total_word, total_word1, total_word2, total_word3) * 1.1
V_max <- max(vocab_size, vocab_size1, vocab_size2, vocab_size3) * 1.1
```


# Create dataframe

```{r}
# create each data frame
# Create data frame for Heaps' law curves
heaps_data <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat, beta_hat)),
    corpus = "pubmed"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat1, beta_hat1)),
    corpus = "gptneo125m"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat2, beta_hat2)),
    corpus = "gptneo1.3b"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat3, beta_hat3)),
    corpus = "gptneo2.7b"
  )
)

# Ensure "corpus" is a factor with levels matching COLORS
heaps_data$corpus <- factor(heaps_data$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))
```


# JSON config 
```{r}
data_dir <- here()
open_files <- c(
pubmed     = "PubMed_human_Open.json",
gptneo125m = "PubMed_gptneo-125M_fewshot_Open.json",
gptneo1.3b = "PubMed_gptneo-1.3B_fewshot_Open.json",
gptneo2.7b = "PubMed_gptneo-2.7B_fewshot_Open.json"
)

RAW_CHECKPOINTS <- 1200

read_tokens_from_json <- function(path) {
x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(path)
unlist(x, use.names = FALSE)
}

empirical_v_curve <- function(tokens, checkpoints = 1000, max_tokens = NULL) {
if (!is.null(max_tokens)) {
tokens <- tokens[seq_len(min(max_tokens, length(tokens)))]
}
N   <- length(tokens)
idx <- unique(round(seq(1, N, length.out = checkpoints)))

seen   <- new.env(hash = TRUE, parent = emptyenv())
vcount <- 0L
V      <- integer(length(idx))
j      <- 1L

for (i in seq_len(N)) {
tk <- tokens[[i]]
if (!exists(tk, envir = seen, inherits = FALSE)) {
assign(tk, TRUE, envir = seen)
vcount <- vcount + 1L
}
if (i == idx[j]) {
V[j] <- vcount
j <- j + 1L
if (j > length(idx)) break
}
}

tibble::tibble(n = idx, V = V)
}

```


# Build empirical curves
```{r}
raw_list <- list()

for (nm in names(open_files)) {
path <- file.path(data_dir, open_files[[nm]])
cat("Reading:", path, "\n")

toks <- read_tokens_from_json(path)

raw_list[[nm]] <- empirical_v_curve(
tokens      = toks,
checkpoints = RAW_CHECKPOINTS
) %>%
mutate(corpus = nm)
}

raw_open <- bind_rows(raw_list)
raw_open$corpus <- factor(raw_open$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))

```


# Heaps' law curves (fits) 

```{r}
heaps_data <- rbind(
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word,  length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word,  length.out = 1000), alpha_hat,  beta_hat)),
corpus = "pubmed"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat1, beta_hat1)),
corpus = "gptneo125m"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat2, beta_hat2)),
corpus = "gptneo1.3b"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat3, beta_hat3)),
corpus = "gptneo2.7b"
)
)

heaps_data$corpus <- factor(heaps_data$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))
```


# Axis limits (based on both raw + fits)

```{r}
n_max <- max(
total_word, total_word1, total_word2, total_word3,
raw_open$n
) * 1.1

V_max <- max(
vocab_size, vocab_size1, vocab_size2, vocab_size3,
raw_open$V
) * 1.1
```


# Plot

```{r}
p <- ggplot()  +
  # Heaps' law fits with color by corpus
  geom_line(
    data = heaps_data,
    aes(x = n, y = V, color = corpus),
    linewidth = 1.3
  )+
  # empirical curves (all corpora) in gray
  geom_line(
    data = raw_open,
    aes(
      x = n,
      y = V,
      group = corpus,
      color = "Empirical Data"   
    ),
    linewidth = 0.6,
    linetype = 2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position      = c(0.99, 0.01),
    legend.justification = c("right", "bottom"),
    legend.text          = element_text(size = rel(0.75)),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = NA),
    plot.margin          = unit(c(1, 1, 1, 1), "cm")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Open vocabulary setting",
    x     = "Corpus size",
    y     = "Vocabulary size",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    # first "Empirical data", then your 4 corpora
    breaks = c("Empirical Data", names(COLORS)),
    values = c(
      "Empirical Data" = "grey35",  # gray for raw curves
      COLORS                        # your original colors for fits
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Empirical data",
      bquote("PubMed Abstracts:" ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat)  ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 125M:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat1) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat1)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 1.3B:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat2) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat2)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 2.7B:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat3) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat3))
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, n_max),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)),
    labels = scientific
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, V_max),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  )

p
```


# Save to PDF

```{r}
ggsave(
plot     = p,
filename = here("pubmed-open-vocab.pdf"),
device   = "pdf",
width    = 6,
height   = 6,
units    = "in",
dpi      = 320,
limitsize = FALSE
)

```






# Closed vocabulary setting

```{r}
# Hard code estimate alpha and beta values

# PubMed Abstracts
alpha_hat <- round(418.6412 , 4)
beta_hat <- round(0.2935, 4)
vocab_size <- 42796
total_word <- 7430935
  
# GPT-Neo 125m
alpha_hat1 <- round(225.0053 , 4)
beta_hat1 <- round(0.3090 , 4)
vocab_size1 <- 27485
total_word1 <- 5898959

# GPT-Neo 1.3B
alpha_hat2 <- round(275.0182 , 4)
beta_hat2 <- round(0.3025, 4)
vocab_size2 <- 32815
total_word2 <- 7667967

# GPT-Neo 2.7B
alpha_hat3 <- round(312.8286 , 4)
beta_hat3 <- round(0.2989, 4)
vocab_size3 <- 34454
total_word3 <- 7131124

# Hard code collection size and vocab size limits
n_max <- max(total_word, total_word1, total_word2, total_word3) * 1.1
V_max <- max(vocab_size, vocab_size1, vocab_size2, vocab_size3) * 1.1
```


```{r}
# Create data frame for Heaps' law curves
heaps_data <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat, beta_hat)),
    corpus = "pubmed"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat1, beta_hat1)),
    corpus = "gptneo125m"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat2, beta_hat2)),
    corpus = "gptneo1.3b"
  ),
  data.frame(
    n = c(0, seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000)),
    V = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat3, beta_hat3)),
    corpus = "gptneo2.7b"
  )
)

# Ensure "corpus" is a factor with levels matching COLORS
heaps_data$corpus <- factor(heaps_data$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))
```



# JSON config

```{r}
data_dir <- here()

open_files <- c(
pubmed     = "PubMed_human_Close.json",
gptneo125m = "PubMed_gptneo-125M_fewshot_Close.json",
gptneo1.3b = "PubMed_gptneo-1.3B_fewshot_Close.json",
gptneo2.7b = "PubMed_gptneo-2.7B_fewshot_Close.json"
)

RAW_CHECKPOINTS <- 1200

read_tokens_from_json <- function(path) {
x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(path)
unlist(x, use.names = FALSE)
}

empirical_v_curve <- function(tokens, checkpoints = 1000, max_tokens = NULL) {
if (!is.null(max_tokens)) {
tokens <- tokens[seq_len(min(max_tokens, length(tokens)))]
}
N   <- length(tokens)
idx <- unique(round(seq(1, N, length.out = checkpoints)))

seen   <- new.env(hash = TRUE, parent = emptyenv())
vcount <- 0L
V      <- integer(length(idx))
j      <- 1L

for (i in seq_len(N)) {
tk <- tokens[[i]]
if (!exists(tk, envir = seen, inherits = FALSE)) {
assign(tk, TRUE, envir = seen)
vcount <- vcount + 1L
}
if (i == idx[j]) {
V[j] <- vcount
j <- j + 1L
if (j > length(idx)) break
}
}

tibble::tibble(n = idx, V = V)
}

```


# Build empirical curves 

```{r}
raw_list <- list()
for (nm in names(open_files)) {
path <- file.path(data_dir, open_files[[nm]])
cat("Reading:", path, "\n")

toks <- read_tokens_from_json(path)

raw_list[[nm]] <- empirical_v_curve(
tokens      = toks,
checkpoints = RAW_CHECKPOINTS
) %>%
mutate(corpus = nm)
}

raw_open <- bind_rows(raw_list)
raw_open$corpus <- factor(raw_open$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))

```


# Heaps' law curves (fits) – Close vocab

```{r}
heaps_data <- rbind(
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word,  length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word,  length.out = 1000), alpha_hat,  beta_hat)),
corpus = "pubmed"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word1, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat1, beta_hat1)),
corpus = "gptneo125m"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word2, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat2, beta_hat2)),
corpus = "gptneo1.3b"
),
data.frame(
n      = c(0, seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000)),
V      = c(0, heaps_law(seq(1, total_word3, length.out = 1000), alpha_hat3, beta_hat3)),
corpus = "gptneo2.7b"
)
)
heaps_data$corpus <- factor(heaps_data$corpus, levels = names(COLORS))
```


# Axis limits (based on both raw + fits)

```{r}
n_max <- max(
total_word, total_word1, total_word2, total_word3,
raw_open$n
) * 1.1

V_max <- max(
vocab_size, vocab_size1, vocab_size2, vocab_size3,
raw_open$V
) * 1.1
```


# Plot

```{r}
p <- ggplot() +
  # Heaps' law fits with color by corpus
  geom_line(
    data = heaps_data,
    aes(x = n, y = V, color = corpus),
    linewidth = 1.3
  )  +
  # empirical curves (all corpora) in gray
  geom_line(
    data = raw_open,
    aes(
      x = n,
      y = V,
      group = corpus,
      color = "Empirical data"  
    ),
    linewidth = 0.6,
    linetype = 2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position      = c(0.99, 0.01),
    legend.justification = c("right", "bottom"),
    legend.text          = element_text(size = rel(0.75)),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = NA),
    plot.margin          = unit(c(1, 1, 1, 1), "cm")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Closed vocabulary setting",
    x     = "Corpus size",
    y     = "Vocabulary size",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    # first "Empirical data", then your 4 corpora
    breaks = c("Empirical data", names(COLORS)),
    values = c(
      "Empirical data" = "grey35",  # gray for raw curves
      COLORS                        # your original colors for fits
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Empirical data",
      bquote("PubMed Abstracts:" ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat)  ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 125M:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat1) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat1)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 1.3B:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat2) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat2)),
      bquote("GPT-Neo 2.7B:"     ~ hat(alpha) == .(alpha_hat3) ~ ", " ~ hat(beta) == .(beta_hat3))
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, n_max),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)),
    labels = scientific
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, V_max),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  )

p
```

# Save to PDF

```{r}
ggsave(
plot     = p,
filename = here("pubmed-closed-vocab.pdf"),
device   = "pdf",
width    = 6,
height   = 6,
units    = "in",
dpi      = 320,
limitsize = FALSE
)
```
